timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: First Battle of Panipat
description: Babur defeats Sultan Ibrahim Lodi and opens the way for Mughal rule in North India.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Babur occupies Delhi and Agra
description: After Panipat, Babur takes the main centres of the Delhi Sultanate and establishes himself as emperor.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Babur wins the Battle of Khanwa
description: Babur defeats Rana Sanga, consolidating Mughal power against a major Rajput coalition.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Humayun succeeds Babur
description: Babur dies and his son Humayun inherits the Mughal throne.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Sher Shah Suri defeats Humayun
description: Humayun loses power after defeat at Kannauj, interrupting Mughal rule in India.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Humayun recaptures Delhi
description: With Safavid support and military recovery, Humayun restores Mughal authority in Delhi.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Akbar succeeds Humayun
description: Humayun dies and the young Akbar becomes emperor under the regency of Bairam Khan.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Second Battle of Panipat
description: Akbar's forces defeat Hemu, securing the restored Mughal state.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Akbar begins durable Rajput alliance policy
description: Akbar deepens political integration with Rajput elites, a major step in imperial consolidation.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Fatehpur Sikri becomes imperial capital
description: Akbar establishes Fatehpur Sikri as a new Mughal capital and court centre.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Battle of Haldighati
description: Mughal forces defeat Maharana Pratap's army, advancing imperial influence in Rajasthan.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Akbar introduces Din-i Ilahi
description: Akbar promotes an eclectic imperial religious initiative associated with his policy of broad toleration.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Jahangir succeeds Akbar
description: Akbar dies and Jahangir ascends the Mughal throne.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Shah Jahan is crowned emperor
description: After a succession struggle, Shah Jahan begins a reign associated with imperial splendour and monumental building.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Construction of the Taj Mahal begins
description: Shah Jahan commissions the mausoleum for Mumtaz Mahal, creating the empire's most famous monument.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Aurangzeb seizes the throne
description: Aurangzeb deposes his father Shah Jahan after a war of succession and becomes emperor.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Shivaji is crowned king
description: The Maratha coronation marks a growing regional challenge to Mughal supremacy in India.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Aurangzeb dies
description: Aurangzeb's death triggers succession conflict and accelerates the fragmentation of Mughal authority.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Nadir Shah sacks Delhi
description: The Persian invasion devastates Delhi and exposes the weakness of the late Mughal state.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Battle of Plassey shifts power toward the East India Company
description: British victory in Bengal weakens the Mughal political order and transforms the balance of power in India.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: British forces capture Delhi
description: The East India Company takes Delhi during the Second Anglo-Maratha War and places the emperor under its control.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: Delhi falls during the Indian Rebellion
description: British forces retake Delhi, effectively ending the political role of Bahadur Shah II in the revolt.

timeline_description: Major political, military, and cultural milestones in the rise, expansion, and fall of the Mughal Empire in South Asia.
title: British Crown abolishes Mughal sovereignty
description: After the rebellion, the British government formally ends Mughal rule and transfers power from the East India Company to the Crown.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: German Workers' Party is founded
description: The small nationalist party that would become the Nazi Party is established in Munich.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Party is renamed NSDAP
description: The German Workers' Party adopts the name National Socialist German Workers' Party and announces its programme.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Beer Hall Putsch begins
description: Hitler and his supporters attempt to seize power in Munich in a failed coup.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Nazi Party is refounded
description: After a temporary ban following the failed putsch, Hitler re-establishes the Nazi Party.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Bamberg Conference centralizes Hitler's control
description: Hitler asserts ideological and organizational authority over competing factions inside the party.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Great Depression destabilizes Weimar Germany
description: Economic crisis helps the Nazis expand support through mass unemployment and political polarization.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Nazis become largest Reichstag party
description: In the July election, the Nazi Party wins the biggest share of seats in parliament.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Hitler is appointed chancellor
description: President Paul von Hindenburg names Adolf Hitler chancellor of Germany.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Reichstag fire
description: The burning of the Reichstag is used by the Nazis to intensify repression of political opponents.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Enabling Act is passed
description: The law gives Hitler's government sweeping dictatorial powers and destroys constitutional government.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Nazi Party becomes the only legal party
description: Germany is formally turned into a one-party state under Nazi rule.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Night of the Long Knives
description: Hitler orders a purge of perceived rivals, consolidating his control over the regime and army.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Hitler becomes Führer
description: After Hindenburg dies, Hitler merges the offices of chancellor and president.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Nuremberg Laws are enacted
description: The regime strips Jews of citizenship and codifies racial discrimination in law.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Rhineland is remilitarized
description: German troops enter the demilitarized Rhineland in defiance of the Versailles settlement.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Anschluss with Austria
description: Nazi Germany annexes Austria, expanding the Reich without military resistance from major powers.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Kristallnacht
description: State-backed anti-Jewish violence destroys synagogues, businesses, and homes across Germany and Austria.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Germany invades Poland
description: The invasion of Poland begins the Second World War in Europe.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Operation Barbarossa begins
description: Germany invades the Soviet Union, radically widening the war and intensifying mass violence.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Wannsee Conference
description: Senior Nazi officials coordinate plans for the deportation and murder of European Jews.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: German defeat at Stalingrad is completed
description: The surrender at Stalingrad marks a decisive turning point against Nazi Germany.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Allied landings in Normandy
description: The D-Day invasion opens a major western front against Nazi Germany.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: July 20 plot fails
description: An assassination attempt on Hitler fails and is followed by a harsh crackdown.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Hitler dies in Berlin
description: Adolf Hitler commits suicide as Soviet forces close in on the capital.

timeline_description: Key events in the rise, rule, expansion, radicalization, and collapse of Nazi Germany.
title: Germany surrenders in Europe
description: The Third Reich collapses with Germany's unconditional surrender.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Kennedy commits the United States to a Moon landing
description: President John F. Kennedy tells Congress that the nation should land a man on the Moon and return him safely to Earth before the decade is out.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: NASA awards the Saturn V contract
description: Boeing is selected to build the Saturn V first stage, a central step in developing the Moon rocket.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: AS-201 flies the first uncrewed Apollo spacecraft test
description: The mission tests major elements of the Apollo command and service module in space.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: AS-203 launches
description: The mission studies the behaviour of liquid hydrogen in orbit for the Saturn V upper stage.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: AS-202 launches
description: A second major uncrewed command and service module test further validates Apollo hardware.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 1 crew dies in launch pad fire
description: A cabin fire during a ground test kills Gus Grissom, Ed White, and Roger Chaffee and forces a major redesign.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 4 conducts first uncrewed Saturn V flight
description: NASA successfully tests the giant Moon rocket for the first time.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 5 tests the lunar module
description: The first uncrewed flight of the lunar module validates its engine and staging systems.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 6 flies the second uncrewed Saturn V mission
description: Despite technical problems, the mission advances Saturn V qualification for crewed flight.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 7 becomes the first crewed Apollo mission
description: The eleven-day Earth-orbit mission successfully tests the redesigned spacecraft.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 8 launches to lunar orbit
description: Apollo 8 becomes the first crewed mission to travel to the Moon and orbit it.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 9 tests the lunar module in Earth orbit
description: The mission proves that the lunar module can operate with astronauts aboard.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 10 rehearses the lunar landing mission
description: The crew performs a full dress rehearsal in lunar orbit short of landing.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 11 launches
description: The first lunar landing mission departs Earth atop a Saturn V rocket.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 11 lands on the Moon
description: Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin land at Tranquility Base while Michael Collins remains in lunar orbit.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 12 completes a precise lunar landing
description: The second crewed lunar landing demonstrates improved mission accuracy and surface operations.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 13 launches
description: The mission begins as the third planned lunar landing expedition.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 13 returns safely after in-flight explosion
description: NASA aborts the landing but brings the crew home alive after a major spacecraft emergency.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 14 lands on the Moon
description: The mission resumes lunar surface exploration after the Apollo 13 accident.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 15 begins extended lunar exploration
description: Apollo 15 introduces the lunar rover and greatly expands scientific fieldwork on the Moon.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 16 lands in the Descartes Highlands
description: The fifth crewed lunar landing gathers new geological data from the highlands.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 17 lands on the Moon
description: The final Apollo lunar landing mission brings geologist Harrison Schmitt to the Moon.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo 17 splashes down
description: The mission ends the era of Apollo lunar flights.

timeline_description: A chronological record of the planning, testing, missions, and legacy milestones of NASA's Apollo lunar programme.
title: Apollo-Soyuz docking marks the programme's epilogue
description: An Apollo spacecraft docks with a Soviet Soyuz in a mission symbolizing détente and the end of the Apollo era.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Mendel describes particulate inheritance
description: Gregor Mendel's pea experiments establish the basic principles of heredity that later underpin genetics and genomics.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Miescher isolates nuclein
description: Friedrich Miescher identifies nuclein, the substance later recognized as DNA.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Chromosome theory of inheritance emerges
description: Walter Sutton and Theodor Boveri connect heredity with chromosomes.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Morgan links genes to chromosomes
description: Thomas Hunt Morgan's work with fruit flies provides strong evidence that genes are carried on chromosomes.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: DNA is identified as hereditary material
description: Avery, MacLeod, and McCarty show that DNA carries genetic information.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Hershey-Chase experiment confirms DNA's role
description: Experiments with bacteriophages reinforce the conclusion that DNA is the genetic material.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Double-helix structure of DNA is proposed
description: James Watson and Francis Crick publish the double-helix model, drawing on crucial work by Rosalind Franklin and Maurice Wilkins.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Human chromosome number is corrected to 46
description: Joe Hin Tjio and Albert Levan establish the correct human chromosome count.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: The genetic code begins to be deciphered
description: Marshall Nirenberg and colleagues take major steps toward linking nucleotide triplets to amino acids.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Restriction enzymes transform molecular biology
description: The use of restriction enzymes creates essential tools for DNA analysis and genome research.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Recombinant DNA methods are developed
description: Paul Berg and others pioneer techniques for combining DNA from different sources.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Sanger sequencing is introduced
description: Frederick Sanger's DNA sequencing method becomes foundational for modern genomics.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: PCR is invented
description: Kary Mullis devises the polymerase chain reaction, enabling rapid amplification of DNA.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Human Genome Project begins
description: An international effort launches to map and sequence the human genome.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: First bacterial genome is completed
description: The genome of Haemophilus influenzae becomes the first complete genome sequence of a free-living organism.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Yeast genome is completed
description: Saccharomyces cerevisiae becomes the first eukaryote with a fully sequenced genome.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: C. elegans genome is published
description: The nematode becomes the first multicellular animal with a sequenced genome.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Draft human genome is published
description: Landmark publications present the first draft sequence of the human genome.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: Human Genome Project is completed
description: Researchers announce the successful completion of the Human Genome Project.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: International HapMap Project publishes results
description: The project creates a major resource for studying human genetic variation.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: 1000 Genomes Project is launched
description: An international consortium begins large-scale sequencing to catalogue human genetic variation.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: CRISPR is adapted for genome editing
description: Researchers show that CRISPR-Cas9 can be programmed for targeted gene editing.

timeline_description: Major scientific milestones in the emergence of genomics, from heredity research to whole-genome sequencing and genome editing.
title: First complete gapless human genome sequence is announced
description: The Telomere-to-Telomere consortium reports the first truly complete human genome sequence.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: X-rays are discovered
description: Wilhelm Conrad Röntgen's discovery opens a new era in atomic and radiation science.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Radioactivity is discovered
description: Henri Becquerel discovers natural radioactivity, a foundational step in nuclear science.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Polonium and radium are isolated
description: Marie and Pierre Curie identify highly radioactive elements central to early atomic research.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Einstein publishes mass-energy equivalence
description: The equation E=mc² provides a theoretical basis for the enormous energy stored in matter.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Neutron is discovered
description: James Chadwick discovers the neutron, enabling later work on nuclear reactions and reactors.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Fermi advances neutron research
description: Enrico Fermi demonstrates the importance of slow neutrons in inducing nuclear reactions.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Nuclear fission is discovered experimentally
description: Otto Hahn and Fritz Strassmann identify fission products from uranium experiments.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Fission is explained theoretically
description: Lise Meitner and Otto Frisch interpret uranium splitting as nuclear fission.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: First controlled nuclear chain reaction
description: Chicago Pile-1 achieves the first self-sustaining controlled nuclear chain reaction.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: EBR-I generates electricity from nuclear power
description: Experimental Breeder Reactor I produces the first usable electricity from nuclear energy.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Atoms for Peace address is delivered
description: U.S. President Dwight D. Eisenhower calls for peaceful international uses of atomic energy.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Obninsk begins grid-connected nuclear generation
description: The Soviet plant at Obninsk becomes the first nuclear power station to supply electricity to a grid.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: First UN conference on peaceful uses of atomic energy opens
description: The Geneva conference marks the global spread of civilian nuclear knowledge.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Calder Hall opens
description: Britain's Calder Hall becomes the world's first full-scale commercial nuclear power station.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: IAEA is established
description: The International Atomic Energy Agency begins work as the main international nuclear oversight body.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Windscale fire
description: A reactor fire in Britain becomes one of the first major nuclear accidents.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Shippingport starts operation
description: Shippingport becomes the first large-scale commercial nuclear power plant in the United States.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty opens for signature
description: The NPT links nuclear technology governance with non-proliferation and peaceful use commitments.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Oil crisis boosts interest in nuclear power
description: The global energy crisis encourages many countries to expand nuclear electricity programmes.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Three Mile Island accident
description: A partial core meltdown in Pennsylvania reshapes public debate and regulation of nuclear power.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Chernobyl disaster
description: Explosion and fire at Reactor 4 create the worst accident in nuclear power history.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: World Association of Nuclear Operators is formed
description: Utilities create WANO to strengthen international operational safety after Chernobyl.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: IAEA and Mohamed ElBaradei receive the Nobel Peace Prize
description: The award recognizes international efforts to prevent military misuse of nuclear energy while promoting safe peaceful use.

timeline_description: A history of the scientific discoveries, reactor milestones, institutions, and accidents that shaped nuclear energy.
title: Fukushima Daiichi disaster
description: An earthquake and tsunami trigger reactor failures and a global reassessment of nuclear safety.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Portuguese sailors seize captives on the West African coast
description: Early Portuguese raiding and trading in captives marks the beginning of Europe's Atlantic slave traffic.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Elmina Castle is founded
description: Portugal establishes Elmina on the Gold Coast, later a major fort in the Atlantic slave trade.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Charles V authorizes direct slave shipments to the Americas
description: The Spanish crown permits direct transportation of enslaved Africans across the Atlantic to Spanish America.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: John Hawkins makes England's first major slaving voyage
description: The English enter the transatlantic slave trade on a significant scale.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Africans arrive in English Virginia
description: A ship brings captive Africans to Virginia, a landmark moment in English colonial slavery in North America.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: England captures Jamaica
description: The island later becomes a major plantation colony deeply tied to the slave trade.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Royal African Company is chartered
description: The English crown charters a company that becomes central to Britain's slave trading system.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: English slave trade is opened beyond company monopoly
description: The end of the Royal African Company monopoly expands British private participation in the trade.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Britain gains the asiento
description: The Treaty of Utrecht gives Britain a contract to supply enslaved Africans to Spanish America.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: British abolition society is founded
description: The Society for Effecting the Abolition of the Slave Trade begins organized antislavery campaigning in Britain.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Haitian Revolution begins
description: The uprising in Saint-Domingue reshapes the Atlantic slave system and strengthens abolitionist pressure.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Denmark moves to abolish its transatlantic slave trade
description: Denmark becomes the first European state to legislate an end to the trade, with implementation set for 1803.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Danish ban on the slave trade takes effect
description: Denmark's earlier legislation against the transatlantic trade becomes operational.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Britain abolishes the slave trade
description: Parliament passes the Abolition of the Slave Trade Act, ending British participation in the trade.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: United States bans the importation of enslaved people
description: The federal ban takes effect on the earliest date permitted by the U.S. Constitution.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Congress of Vienna condemns the trade
description: European powers denounce the slave trade, giving abolition diplomacy wider international backing.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Amistad revolt draws global attention
description: Captive Africans seize control of La Amistad, creating a major legal and political antislavery case.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Webster-Ashburton Treaty expands anti-slave-trade patrols
description: Britain and the United States agree to cooperate more closely against the Atlantic trade.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Last documented illegal transatlantic slaving voyages arrive in Cuba
description: By the mid-1860s the Atlantic trade is nearing its final end, though illegal trafficking persists.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Atlantic Slave Trade
timeline_description: A chronological overview of the rise, expansion, resistance, and abolition of the transatlantic slave trade.
title: Brazil abolishes slavery
description: The Lei Áurea ends slavery in the last major slave society in the Americas tied to the Atlantic system.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: John Lennon meets Paul McCartney
description: At a Quarrymen performance in Liverpool, Lennon meets McCartney, starting the partnership that would define the Beatles.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: George Harrison joins the group
description: Harrison joins the evolving Quarrymen lineup, helping form the future Beatles core.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: The Beatles name and Hamburg period begin
description: The group adopts the Beatles name and begins formative club residencies in Hamburg.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: Brian Epstein sees the Beatles at the Cavern Club
description: Epstein's interest leads to his management of the band and a professional breakthrough.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: The Beatles audition for Decca
description: The band makes a famous but unsuccessful audition for Decca Records.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: Ringo Starr joins the Beatles
description: Ringo Starr replaces Pete Best, creating the classic four-member lineup.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: Love Me Do is released
description: The Beatles issue their first single on Parlophone.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: Please Please Me album is released
description: The band's debut LP launches its national breakthrough in Britain.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: Beatlemania surges after London Palladium appearance
description: A major television appearance helps turn the band into a mass cultural phenomenon.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: The Beatles appear on The Ed Sullivan Show
description: Their U.S. television debut becomes a defining moment in the British Invasion.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: A Hard Day's Night premieres
description: The feature film extends the Beatles' global cultural reach beyond records and concerts.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: The Beatles play Shea Stadium
description: The concert becomes an iconic landmark in the history of stadium rock.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: Final commercial concert
description: The Beatles end regular touring with a performance at Candlestick Park.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band is released
description: The album becomes one of the defining works of popular music and studio experimentation.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: All You Need Is Love is broadcast worldwide
description: The Beatles perform the song on Our World, the first live global television link.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: The Beatles release the White Album
description: The sprawling double album reflects the band's widening musical range and internal tensions.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: Rooftop concert takes place
description: The Beatles give their last public performance on the roof of Apple Corps headquarters.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: Abbey Road is released in the UK
description: The album becomes the last one the band records together in full studio collaboration.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: McCartney announces his departure
description: Paul McCartney's public statement confirms the band's breakup in practical terms.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: Let It Be is released
description: The Beatles issue their final studio album after the breakup announcement.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: John Lennon is killed
description: Lennon's death shocks the world and transforms the memory of the band and its legacy.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: The Beatles are inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame
description: The group's historic influence is formally recognized by the Hall of Fame.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: Anthology 1 and Free as a Bird are released
description: The Beatles reunite in archival form around a new recording built from a Lennon demo.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: George Harrison dies
description: Harrison's death marks the loss of the band's lead guitarist and songwriter.

timeline_description: The major performances, recordings, cultural breakthroughs, breakup, and legacy milestones of the Beatles.
title: Now and Then is released
description: Marketed as the final Beatles song, the track completes a Lennon demo with modern audio restoration.

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Battle of Solferino shocks Europe
description: The carnage of Solferino inspires Henri Dunant and becomes a catalyst for organized humanitarian reform in war.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: A Memory of Solferino is published
description: Henri Dunant's book calls for relief societies and international rules for the wounded in war.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Committee of Five is formed in Geneva
description: The committee that becomes the ICRC is established to pursue Dunant's proposals.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: International conference lays Red Cross foundations
description: Delegates in Geneva endorse principles for volunteer aid societies and battlefield relief.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: First Geneva Convention is adopted
description: States agree on the first lasting treaty of modern international humanitarian law.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: St Petersburg Declaration is adopted
description: The declaration prohibits certain explosive projectiles and strengthens the idea that warfare has legal limits.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: First Hague Convention is adopted
description: States codify important rules on the conduct of war and dispute settlement.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Geneva Convention is revised
description: The original convention for wounded and sick soldiers is updated and expanded.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Hague Conventions are expanded
description: A second Hague Peace Conference broadens treaty law on land and sea warfare.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Geneva Gas Protocol is signed
description: States prohibit the wartime use of asphyxiating, poisonous gases and bacteriological methods.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: POW convention is adopted
description: A new Geneva Convention strengthens legal protection for prisoners of war.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Four Geneva Conventions are adopted
description: Postwar diplomacy produces the core treaties of modern international humanitarian law, including protection for civilians.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Geneva Conventions enter into force
description: The four 1949 conventions become legally operative.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Hague Convention on cultural property is adopted
description: States create treaty protections for cultural property during armed conflict.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Additional Protocols I and II are adopted
description: The protocols update the Geneva Conventions for international and non-international armed conflicts.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Convention on Certain Conventional Weapons is adopted
description: The CCW restricts or bans weapons considered excessively injurious or indiscriminate.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Chemical Weapons Convention is opened for signature
description: The treaty bans chemical weapons and strengthens international regulation of warfare methods.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Ottawa Mine Ban Treaty is signed
description: States adopt a landmark treaty banning anti-personnel landmines.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Rome Statute is adopted
description: The statute creates the legal foundation for the International Criminal Court.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: International Criminal Court begins work
description: The Rome Statute enters into force and the ICC becomes operational.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_description: Major treaties, institutions, and legal turning points in the development of international humanitarian law.
title: Third Additional Protocol is adopted
description: States approve the Red Crystal as an additional protective emblem alongside the Red Cross and Red Crescent.
source_publisher: International Committee of the Red Cross

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Virginia Declaration of Rights is adopted
description: Virginia issues a foundational statement of rights that influences later American constitutional thought.
source_publisher: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Articles of Confederation are approved by Congress
description: The Continental Congress adopts the first framework of national government for the United States.
source_publisher: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Articles of Confederation take effect
description: Maryland's ratification brings the Articles into force.
source_publisher: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Annapolis Convention meets
description: Delegates call for a broader convention to revise the national political system.
source_publisher: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Constitutional Convention convenes
description: Delegates gather in Philadelphia to revise the Articles but ultimately draft a new constitution.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: U.S. Constitution is signed
description: The convention approves and signs the proposed Constitution.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Delaware becomes the first state to ratify
description: Delaware is the first state to approve the Constitution.
source_publisher: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: New Hampshire becomes the ninth ratifying state
description: The Constitution secures the ratifications needed to take effect under its own terms.
source_publisher: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: George Washington is inaugurated
description: The new constitutional government begins operating under its first president.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Congress proposes the Bill of Rights
description: The First Congress sends twelve amendments to the states in response to ratification-era concerns.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Bill of Rights is ratified
description: Ten proposed amendments become the first ten amendments to the Constitution.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Eleventh Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment limits suits against states in federal court.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Twelfth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment revises presidential election procedures after the election crisis of 1800.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Thirteenth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment abolishes slavery in the United States.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Fourteenth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment establishes birthright citizenship and equal protection principles.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Fifteenth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment prohibits denial of voting rights based on race, colour, or previous condition of servitude.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Sixteenth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment authorizes a federal income tax.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Seventeenth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment provides for direct election of U.S. senators.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Nineteenth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment prohibits denying the vote on the basis of sex.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Twenty-first Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment repeals Prohibition and overturns the Eighteenth Amendment.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Twenty-second Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment limits presidents to two elected terms.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Twenty-third Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment grants presidential electors to the District of Columbia.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Twenty-fourth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment bans poll taxes in federal elections.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Twenty-fifth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment clarifies presidential succession and disability procedures.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Twenty-sixth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment lowers the voting age to eighteen.

timeline_title: Timeline of the United States Constitution
timeline_description: Founding, ratification, and amendment milestones in the history of the United States Constitution.
title: Twenty-seventh Amendment is ratified
description: A long-pending proposal on congressional pay becomes the most recent amendment to the Constitution.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade is signed
description: Twenty-three countries sign the GATT, laying the foundation for the postwar multilateral trading system.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: GATT enters into force provisionally
description: The GATT begins operating as the core framework for multilateral trade rules.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Dillon Round concludes
description: An early GATT negotiation round reduces tariffs among participating economies.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Kennedy Round opens
description: GATT members launch a major negotiating round focused on tariff cuts and trade rules.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Kennedy Round concludes
description: The round ends with substantial tariff reductions and an anti-dumping code.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Tokyo Round begins
description: GATT members open another broad round of negotiations on tariffs and non-tariff barriers.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Tokyo Round concludes
description: The round produces tariff cuts and a set of codes on trade practices.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Uruguay Round is launched
description: Trade ministers open the negotiation round that will eventually create the WTO.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Marrakesh Agreement is signed
description: Governments sign the agreement establishing the World Trade Organization.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: World Trade Organization begins operations
description: The WTO formally replaces the GATT as the main institution governing world trade rules.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: First WTO Ministerial Conference concludes
description: The inaugural ministerial meeting in Singapore sets an early agenda for the new organization.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Second Ministerial Conference opens in Geneva
description: Members meet on the fiftieth anniversary of the multilateral trading system.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Seattle Ministerial collapses without agreement
description: The failed meeting exposes sharp tensions over globalization, agriculture, and labour issues.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Doha Development Agenda is launched
description: Members begin a new round of negotiations aimed in part at development concerns.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: China joins the WTO
description: China's accession becomes one of the most consequential membership expansions in WTO history.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Hong Kong Ministerial reaches limited agreements
description: Members agree on steps including the end of agricultural export subsidies by a future deadline.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Bali Package is adopted
description: WTO members reach the first multilateral agreement since the organization's creation, centred on trade facilitation and development issues.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Nairobi Ministerial adopts key agriculture decisions
description: Members agree to eliminate export subsidies for farm products.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Buenos Aires Ministerial ends without broad consensus
description: The eleventh ministerial highlights ongoing deadlock on major negotiating issues.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Twelfth Ministerial Conference reaches a package of deals
description: Members agree on outcomes including fisheries subsidies, food security, pandemic-related measures, and e-commerce work.

timeline_title: Timeline of the World Trade Organization
timeline_description: Key institutional, negotiating, and ministerial milestones in the history of the WTO and the modern multilateral trading system.
title: Thirteenth Ministerial Conference closes in Abu Dhabi
description: The conference ends with selective outcomes but no major breakthrough on long-standing negotiating issues.
